_comment: Add a _ before an option name to comment it out and it wont be added to the apworld
Using 'goal' as the name of an option let you override some of the generated goal parameters
description: Number of total citizens (adults + students + children) required for victory
'Reach 300 Population' (default) is recommended, but you can lower it for a shorter game. Some checks will be excluded if you do.
Any of these goals can be made easier/faster by starting on easy or medium difficulty.
Settings lower than 300 will put a hard cap on the number of trades, if randomizing trades.

filler_traps._comment: Using values here let you set a 'recommended' filler_trap percentage or even a default percentage

description: This is a Toggle
A simple boolean, aka True or False set the initial value with "default"
This is a Choice
Let the user pick from a list of values
allow_custom_value let a player define their own text value
Currently the only use for Choice in Manual outside of hooks is the goal
This is a Range
Allow the player to specify a value between 'start' and 'end'
if you include a "values" you can define the label for certain values
Currently the only use for Range in Manual outside of hooks is 'filler_trap'
Wood Cutter will always appear in early checks, but if you'd rather start with it enabled, here you go.
Place Wood Cutter in your own world. In multiplayer, this will prevent early BK without the ability to hint.
Blacksmith will always appear in early checks, but if you'd rather start with it enabled, here you go.
The Banished in-game difficulty you intend to start on (defaults to Hard). Affects your starting AP items.
Easy and medium grant immediate access to crops, orchards, and storage barns. Easy also grants immediate access to sheep and pasture.
Easy and medium make the rando drastically more forgiving and are best suited for Banished beginners.
Number of total citizens (adults + students + children) required for victory, and how many years, if any, to sustain it.
300 (default) is recommended, but you can lower it for a shorter game. Some checks will be excluded if you do.
Listed roughly from most to least time taken to goal.
1 year adds approximately 10 minutes of game time if running at 10x speed.
Any of these goals can be made easier/faster by starting on easy or medium difficulty.
TODO put exactly how many checks will be excluded at each threshold
Start with Disasters 'On' when creating your town and randomize 4 items that let you disable each disaster via Debug Mod.
Include locations where you have to trade with the trader, or not. This doesn't affect the 'Make Your First Trade' location.
If including seed/livestock trades, do you want the checks behind those trades hinted?
Turning off these hints might mean trading a ton of your town's resources for not very useful items.
However, keeping them on will reveal about 10% of your locations, which might be too spoilery for some.
If randomizing trades, how many seed trade checks to include, from 1-16. Defaults to 8.
Reach these locations by acquiring any seeds from the trader (repeats allowed).
Note that each seed purchase is a randomized check, and doesn't give you the ability to plant that seed.
Simulates the trade gameplay loop from the vanilla game.
If randomizing trades, how many livestock trade checks to include, from 1-3. Defaults to 2.
Reach these locations by acquiring a pair of animals from the trader.
Simulates the trade gameplay loop from the vanilla game.
Number of in-game years required to sustain the population count from 'Population Required to Win'
Each year adds approximately 10 minutes of game time if running at 10x speed.
Has no effect on check locations.
Default (0 Years) is recommended if your Population goal is 150+.
A lower population setting (ie 50, 100) comboed with a Sustain setting is recommended for a more relaxed game focused on stabilizing vs growing your town.
_Population_Sustain.display_name: How Long to Sustain Population to Win
